response: Based on the provided context, Tymnet was an international data communications network headquartered in San Jose, CA, that utilized virtual call packet switched technology and used X.25, SNA/SDLC, BSC, and ASCII interfaces to connect host computers (servers) at thousands of large companies, educational institutions, and government agencies. It operated both a large public network for dial-up users and a private network business that allowed organizations to build their own dedicated networks.
expected: an international data communications network headquartered in San Jose, CA
